title: Seriphidium barrelieri
taxonomy: Seriphidium barrelieri belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification is based on its characteristics, such as the presence of composite flowers and a taproot. Seriphidium barrelieri is a genus of flowering plants in the tribe Anthemideae. The plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry, rocky areas. In terms of classification, Seriphidium barrelieri is also sometimes referred to as a synonym of Seriphidium scorzoneraefolium subsp. barrelieri.

intro: Seriphidium barrelieri, also known as Artemisia barrelieri, is a perennial shrub native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic activities, making it beneficial for treating digestive issues, skin conditions, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Seriphidium barrelieri include artemisinin, flavonoids, and sesquiterpene lactones, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as an infusion, tincture, or essential oil, which are often used to treat digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections.
